# PowerShell Standards

Standards and templates for all PowerShell scripts generated by the entra-poc-advisor skill.

## Script Structure

Every generated script follows this structure:

```powershell
<#
.SYNOPSIS
    {Brief description of what the script configures}

.DESCRIPTION
    {Detailed description including which Entra Suite features are configured}
    
    Generated by entra-poc-advisor on {date}.
    Scenario: {scenario name or "Custom"}

.PARAMETER TenantId
    The tenant ID to connect to. If omitted, uses the current context.

.PARAMETER PilotGroupName
    Name of the security group containing pilot users. Default: "POC-Pilot-Users"

.PARAMETER WhatIf
    Shows what would be configured without making changes.

.EXAMPLE
    .\{script-name}.ps1 -WhatIf
    Preview all configuration changes without applying them.

.EXAMPLE
    .\{script-name}.ps1 -TenantId "contoso.onmicrosoft.com"
    Apply configuration to the specified tenant.

.NOTES
    This script is idempotent and safe to run multiple times.
    It will NOT delete any existing configuration.
    
    Required modules: Microsoft.Graph
    Required permissions: {list of Graph permission scopes}
    
    Run with -WhatIf first to preview changes.
#>

[CmdletBinding(SupportsShouldProcess)]
param(
    [Parameter(Mandatory = $false)]
    [string]$TenantId,

    [Parameter(Mandatory = $false)]
    [string]$PilotGroupName = "POC-Pilot-Users"

    # Additional parameters as needed
)

# ============================================================================
# Prerequisites Check
# ============================================================================

#region Prerequisites
Write-Host "Checking prerequisites..." -ForegroundColor Cyan

# Check for required PowerShell module
if (-not (Get-Module -ListAvailable -Name "Microsoft.Graph")) {
    Write-Host "ERROR: Microsoft.Graph module not found. Install with:" -ForegroundColor Red
    Write-Host "  Install-Module Microsoft.Graph -Scope CurrentUser" -ForegroundColor Yellow
    exit 1
}

Import-Module Microsoft.Graph
#endregion

# ============================================================================
# Authentication
# ============================================================================

#region Authentication
Write-Host "Connecting to Microsoft Graph..." -ForegroundColor Cyan

$scopes = @(
    # List all required scopes here
)

$connectParams = @{
    Scopes = $scopes
}
if ($TenantId) {
    $connectParams.TenantId = $TenantId
}

Connect-MgGraph @connectParams

# Verify connection
$context = Get-MgContext
if (-not $context) {
    Write-Host "ERROR: Failed to connect to Microsoft Graph." -ForegroundColor Red
    exit 1
}
Write-Host "  Connected to tenant: $($context.TenantId)" -ForegroundColor Green
#endregion

# ============================================================================
# Step 1: {Step Title}
# ============================================================================

#region Step 1
Write-Host "`n[1/{N}] {Step description}..." -ForegroundColor Cyan

try {
    # Check if resource already exists (idempotency)
    $existing = $null
    try {
        $existing = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET -Uri "{endpoint}" -ErrorAction Stop
    } catch {
        # Resource does not exist -- this is expected for new configurations
    }

    if ($null -eq $existing) {
        if ($PSCmdlet.ShouldProcess("{resource description}", "Create")) {
            $body = @{
                # Request body
            } | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 10

            $result = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method POST -Uri "{endpoint}" `
                -Body $body -ContentType "application/json"
            Write-Host "  Created {resource}: $($result.id)" -ForegroundColor Green
        }
    } else {
        Write-Host "  {Resource} already exists (id: $($existing.id)), skipping." -ForegroundColor Yellow
    }
} catch {
    Write-Host "  ERROR: Failed to configure {resource}: $_" -ForegroundColor Red
    Write-Host "  Continuing with remaining steps..." -ForegroundColor Yellow
}
#endregion

# ============================================================================
# Summary
# ============================================================================

#region Summary
Write-Host "`n========================================" -ForegroundColor Green
Write-Host "POC Configuration Complete!" -ForegroundColor Green
Write-Host "========================================" -ForegroundColor Green
Write-Host "`nConfigured components:"
Write-Host "  - {Component 1}"
Write-Host "  - {Component 2}"
Write-Host "`nNext steps:"
Write-Host "  1. {Manual validation step}"
Write-Host "  2. {Testing step}"
Write-Host "`nReview the audit log for details on all changes made."
#endregion
```

## Conventions

### Authentication

- Always use `Connect-MgGraph` with explicit scopes
- Never hardcode credentials or tokens
- Support optional `-TenantId` parameter
- Verify connection before proceeding

### API Calls

- Always use `Invoke-MgGraphRequest` for all Microsoft Graph operations
- Do NOT use `Invoke-RestMethod`, `Invoke-WebRequest`, or legacy cmdlets
- Use the beta endpoint (`/beta/`) for Global Secure Access and newer Entra features
- Use the v1.0 endpoint (`/v1.0/`) for stable features (users, groups, licenses)
- Always specify `-ContentType "application/json"` for POST/PATCH/PUT

### Idempotency

- Before creating any resource, check if it already exists
- Use GET requests to look up existing resources by name or identifier
- If the resource exists, skip creation and log that it was skipped
- Use `try/catch` around existence checks since 404 is expected for new resources

### No Deletions

- NEVER include `Remove-*` cmdlets
- NEVER include DELETE method calls
- NEVER include `Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method DELETE`
- If a resource needs to be replaced, create the new one and log a note that the old one should be manually removed

### Error Handling

```powershell
try {
    $result = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method POST -Uri $uri -Body $body -ContentType "application/json"
    Write-Host "  Success: Created $($result.displayName)" -ForegroundColor Green
} catch {
    $errorMessage = $_.Exception.Message
    if ($_.Exception.Response.StatusCode -eq 403) {
        Write-Host "  ERROR: Insufficient permissions. Required scope: {scope}" -ForegroundColor Red
    } elseif ($_.Exception.Response.StatusCode -eq 409) {
        Write-Host "  SKIPPED: Resource already exists (conflict)." -ForegroundColor Yellow
    } else {
        Write-Host "  ERROR: $errorMessage" -ForegroundColor Red
    }
}
```

### Progress Output

Color coding for `Write-Host`:

| Color | Meaning | Example |
|---|---|---|
| Cyan | Progress/status | `Write-Host "[1/5] Configuring..." -ForegroundColor Cyan` |
| Green | Success | `Write-Host "  Created resource" -ForegroundColor Green` |
| Yellow | Skipped/warning | `Write-Host "  Already exists, skipping" -ForegroundColor Yellow` |
| Red | Error | `Write-Host "  ERROR: Failed" -ForegroundColor Red` |

### WhatIf Support

- Add `[CmdletBinding(SupportsShouldProcess)]` to every script
- Wrap every modification in `$PSCmdlet.ShouldProcess()`
- The ShouldProcess target should describe what is being modified
- The ShouldProcess action should describe the operation

```powershell
if ($PSCmdlet.ShouldProcess("Traffic Forwarding Profile 'POC-Private-Access'", "Create")) {
    # Create the resource
}
```

### Parameters

- Tenant-specific values (group names, user UPNs, IP ranges) are script parameters
- Provide sensible defaults for POC scenarios
- Use `[Parameter(Mandatory = $false)]` for most parameters
- Use `[Parameter(Mandatory = $true)]` only for values with no reasonable default
- Document all parameters in the comment-based help

### Graph Permission Scopes

Common scopes needed for Entra POC configuration:

| Scope | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `NetworkAccess.ReadWrite.All` | Global Secure Access configuration |
| `Policy.ReadWrite.ConditionalAccess` | Conditional Access policies |
| `Application.ReadWrite.All` | App registrations and enterprise apps |
| `Directory.ReadWrite.All` | Directory objects (users, groups) |
| `Policy.Read.All` | Read all policies |
| `AuditLog.Read.All` | Read audit and sign-in logs |
| `Group.ReadWrite.All` | Security group management |

### Script Sections

Use region markers and comment separators for script organization:

```powershell
# ============================================================================
# Section Name
# ============================================================================

#region SectionName
# Section code
#endregion
```

## Validation Script Pattern

For scripts that validate configuration (not create it):

```powershell
$results = @()

# Check component
$status = "Missing"
try {
    $config = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET -Uri "{endpoint}"
    if ($config) {
        $status = "Configured"
        # Additional validation...
    }
} catch {
    $status = "Missing"
}

$results += [PSCustomObject]@{
    Component = "{Component Name}"
    Status    = $status
    Expected  = "{expected value}"
    Current   = "{current value}"
    Action    = "{remediation if needed}"
}

# Output results
$results | Format-Table -AutoSize
```
